title: ocotea beulahiae
intro: Ocotea beulahiae, a species of tree native to the tropical regions of Central and South America, belongs to the family Lauraceae and is also known as 'Bois de Panama' or 'Palo de Panama'. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its essential oils, which contain sesquiterpenes and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Ocotea beulahiae include its potential anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects, which may help alleviate symptoms of anxiety, insomnia, and other health conditions. In herbal preparations, Ocotea beulahiae is often used in aromatherapy, herbal teas, and extracts, where its essential oils can be distilled and used to promote relaxation and well-being.
